name: Hiking in Dana
shortDescription: Walk through Dana's valleys, viewpoints, and village trails with routes suited to different experience levels.
longDescription: Dana is one of Jordan's best places for guided hiking, with landscapes that shift from limestone cliffs to sandstone valleys. Trails can be planned as gentle walks or longer full-day routes, depending on season, fitness, and guide availability.

name: Diving in Aqaba
shortDescription: Explore Red Sea reefs, warm water, and accessible dive sites along Jordan's short but rewarding coastline.
longDescription: Aqaba is suitable for certified divers, beginners, and snorkelers, with coral gardens and underwater sites close to shore. Local operators offer guided dives, training sessions, and boat trips depending on weather and experience.

name: Hot Air Ballooning in Wadi Rum
shortDescription: See Wadi Rum from above as desert cliffs, dunes, and camps catch the early morning light.
longDescription: Hot air balloon flights are typically scheduled around sunrise and depend on wind and seasonal operating conditions. When available, the experience offers a quiet, panoramic view of one of Jordan's most memorable landscapes.

name: Camel Trekking
shortDescription: Travel slowly through desert routes with local guides and a traditional form of transport.
longDescription: Camel trekking offers a quieter way to experience Wadi Rum's open spaces, rock formations, and desert silence. Short rides are easy to combine with jeep tours, while longer routes require more planning and guide support.

name: Petra by Night
shortDescription: Walk the candlelit Siq to the Treasury for an atmospheric evening experience at Petra.
longDescription: Petra by Night is a separate evening program held on selected nights and should be checked in advance. It is best enjoyed as a reflective complement to a daytime visit, when the site's scale and details are easier to explore.

name: Dead Sea Floating
shortDescription: Float in the mineral-rich Dead Sea and enjoy one of Jordan's most relaxing natural experiences.
longDescription: The high salinity makes floating effortless, while many resorts and public beach areas offer access to showers and spa facilities. Visitors should avoid shaving beforehand, protect cuts, and keep water away from the eyes.

name: Jerash Ruins Tour
shortDescription: Tour Jerash's Roman streets, temples, theaters, and plazas with time to understand the city's scale.
longDescription: A guided visit helps bring the archaeological site to life, connecting the Oval Plaza, Cardo, theaters, gates, and temples into one coherent story. Jerash works well as a half-day trip from Amman or as part of a northern Jordan route.

name: Wadi Rum Jeep Tour
shortDescription: Cover Wadi Rum's major desert viewpoints, rock bridges, dunes, and canyons with a local driver-guide.
longDescription: Jeep tours are a practical way to see more of the protected area in limited time while learning from local guides. Routes often include scenic stops, short walks, tea breaks, sunset viewpoints, and transfer to desert camps.
